SEARCH
Package org.Buffer

Class TerminalBuffer

java.lang.Object
org.Buffer.TerminalBuffer
public class TerminalBuffer extends Object
A class implementing a Terminal text buffer for holding the text currently on the screen and that has been scrolled past Stores two buffers for the screen and scrollback content, alongside the current cursor position It also stores the maximum amount of data you can store in the scrollback buffer and the foreground and background colours the screen should default to.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    TerminalBuffer(Integer width, Integer height, Integer scrollMax)
    Constructor for a TerminalBuffer not including paramters for screen colours.
    TerminalBuffer(Integer width, Integer height, Integer scrollMax, Colour backgroundColour, Colour foregroundColour)
    Constructor for a TerminalBuffer including paramters for screen colours

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    clearEntireBuffer()
    Clears all data in screen and scrollback buffers and resets the cursor position
    boolean
    clearLine()
    Clears the last line in the buffer and rebuilds the screen if necessary.
    void
    clearScreen()
    Clears the lines from the screen.
    boolean
    createNewLine()
    Adds an empty line to the bottom of the screen and removes extra lines if we are over the scrollback buffer.
    boolean
    deleteText()
    Moves the cursor left one position and removes the character at the cursor's new position If the cursor lands on a wide char, removes both characters in the char
    boolean

    Fills a terminal line with the specified character starting from the cursor's current screen position e.g.

    Fills a terminal line with the specified character starting from the cursor's current screen position e.g.

    insertText(Character text)
    Inserts text at the mouse cursor's position only if the cursor is at the bottom of the screen and scrollback Moves the cursor to the right w times, where w is the width of the input Character If the cursor starts at the end of a wide character, moves one space to the left before writing If inserting the input at the current position would overlap with other wide characters, erase them first
    void

    Moves the cursor's x position by some amount of steps.
    void

    Moves the cursor's y position by some amount of steps.

    printScrollLine()

    scroll(Integer spaces)
    Moves the bottom index of the screen by some number of spaces Clears the current screen buffer and rebuilds it using the new bottom of the screen as a reference into the scrollback
    boolean
    setBackgroundColourAtCursorPos(Colour colour)
    Sets the background colour at the mouse cursor's current position only if the cursor is at the bottom of the screen and scrollback.
    void
    setCursorX(Integer val)
    Sets a cursor's x position to the specified position, clamped between [0, logical line width) If the cursor ends at a cell with TrailFlag WIDE_END, move one cell to the left
    void
    setCursorY(Integer val)
    Sets a cursor's y position to the specified position, clamped between [0, scrollback height) If the new position is on a line off of the screen, scroll
    boolean
    setForegroundColourAtCursorPos(Colour colour)
    Sets the foreground colour at the mouse cursor's current position only if the cursor is at the bottom of the screen and scrollback.

    Sets the height of the screen and rebuilds the layout

    setScrollMaximum(Integer val)
    Sets the maximum amount of lines that can be stored in the scrollback buffer
    boolean
    setStyleAtCursorPos(Style style, boolean val)
    Sets the style at the mouse cursor's current position only if the cursor is at the bottom of the screen and scrollback.
    void

    Sets the width of the screen and rebuilds the layout

  • Constructor Details

    • TerminalBuffer

      public TerminalBuffer(Integer width, Integer height, Integer scrollMax, Colour backgroundColour, Colour foregroundColour)
      Constructor for a TerminalBuffer including paramters for screen colours
      Parameters:
      width - the width of the screen represented by the buffer in characters
      height - the height of the screen represented by the buffer in lines
      scrollMax - the maximum number of lines the scrollback buffers can store. If the input is negative, it will be assumed to be Integer.MAX_VALUE
      backgroundColour - the background colour of the screen
      foregroundColour - the foreground colour of the screen

    • TerminalBuffer

      public TerminalBuffer(Integer width, Integer height, Integer scrollMax)
      Constructor for a TerminalBuffer not including paramters for screen colours. This will default the background and foreground colours to black and white respectively
      Parameters:
      width - the width of the screen represented by the buffer in characters
      height - the height of the screen represented by the buffer in lines
      scrollMax - the maximum number of lines the scrollback buffers can store. If the input is negative, it will be assumed to be Long.MAX_VALUE

  • Method Details

    • getScreenCursorX

      public Integer getScreenCursorX()
      Returns:
      the on-screen x position of the cursor

    • getScreenCursorY

      public Integer getScreenCursorY()
      Returns:
      the on-screen y position of the cursor

    • getScrollMaximum

      public Integer getScrollMaximum()
      Returns:
      the maximum amount of lines that can be stored in the scrollback buffer

    • setScrollMaximum

      public void setScrollMaximum(Integer val)
      Sets the maximum amount of lines that can be stored in the scrollback buffer
      Parameters:
      val - the new maximum amount of lines that can be stored in the scrollback buffer

    • getCursorX

      public Integer getCursorX()
      Returns:
      the x position of the cursor in the scrollback

    • getCursorY

      public Integer getCursorY()
      Returns:
      the y position of the cursor in the scrollback

    • getScreenBackgroundColour

      public Colour getScreenBackgroundColour()
      Returns:
      the background colour the text in this screen defaults to

    • getScreenForegroundColour

      public Colour getScreenForegroundColour()
      Returns:
      the foreground colour the text in this screen defaults to

    • setScreenBackgroundColour

      public void setScreenBackgroundColour(Colour val)
      Parameters:
      val - the new screen backgrouund colour. Does not set inputs of Colour.DEFAULT

    • setScreenForegroundColour

      public void setScreenForegroundColour(Colour val)
      Parameters:
      val - the new screen foreground colour. Does not set inputs of Colour.DEFAULT

    • getHeight

      public Integer getHeight()
      Returns:
      the height of the screen represented by this buffer

    • getWidth

      public Integer getWidth()
      Returns:
      the width of the screen represented by this buffer

    • setHeight

      public void setHeight(Integer val)
      Sets the height of the screen and rebuilds the layout
      Parameters:
      val - the new screen height

    • setWidth

      public void setWidth(Integer val)
      Sets the width of the screen and rebuilds the layout
      Parameters:
      val - the new screen width

    • getScreenLine

      public TerminalLine getScreenLine(int index)
      Returns:
      the TerminalLine at the given index of the screen

    • getScreenSize

      public int getScreenSize()
      Returns:
      the number of TerminalLine actually currently displayed in the screen

    • setCursorX

      public void setCursorX(Integer val)
      Sets a cursor's x position to the specified position, clamped between [0, logical line width) If the cursor ends at a cell with TrailFlag WIDE_END, move one cell to the left
      Parameters:
      val - the new x position to move the cursor to

    • setCursorY

      public void setCursorY(Integer val)
      Sets a cursor's y position to the specified position, clamped between [0, scrollback height) If the new position is on a line off of the screen, scroll
      Parameters:
      val - the new x position to move the cursor to

    • moveCursorX

      public void moveCursorX(Integer val)
      Moves the cursor's x position by some amount of steps. Negative values move to the left, positive values move to the right. The cursor's end position is clamped between [0, line_width), where line_width is the number of characters in the unwrapped line the cursor is on If the cursor ends at a cell with TrailFlag WIDE_END, move one cell in the direction you want to move to
      Parameters:
      val - the number of steps to move

    • moveCursorY

      public void moveCursorY(Integer val)
      Moves the cursor's y position by some amount of steps. If the movement would cause the cursor to move off the screen, scroll The cursor's end position is clamped between [0, number of lines)
      Parameters:
      val - the number of steps to move

    • scroll

      public void scroll(Integer spaces)
      Moves the bottom index of the screen by some number of spaces Clears the current screen buffer and rebuilds it using the new bottom of the screen as a reference into the scrollback
      Parameters:
      spaces - the number of spaces to scroll by. negative means down, positive means up

    • createNewLine

      public boolean createNewLine()
      Adds an empty line to the bottom of the screen and removes extra lines if we are over the scrollback buffer. Moves the cursor down one line, scrolling if necessary

    • clearScreen

      public void clearScreen()
      Clears the lines from the screen. Does not remove anything from the scrollback

    • clearEntireBuffer

      public void clearEntireBuffer()
      Clears all data in screen and scrollback buffers and resets the cursor position

    • insertText

      public boolean insertText(Character text)
      Inserts text at the mouse cursor's position only if the cursor is at the bottom of the screen and scrollback Moves the cursor to the right w times, where w is the width of the input Character If the cursor starts at the end of a wide character, moves one space to the left before writing If inserting the input at the current position would overlap with other wide characters, erase them first
      Parameters:
      text - the new character to add
      Returns:
      true if the text was inserted, false if the cursor is not at the bottom line

    • deleteText

      public boolean deleteText()
      Moves the cursor left one position and removes the character at the cursor's new position If the cursor lands on a wide char, removes both characters in the char
      Returns:
      true on successful removal, false if it is not at the bottom line in the scrollback or if there is no char to erase

    • overwriteText

      public boolean overwriteText(Character text)
      Overwrites text at the mouse cursor's current position only if the cursor is at the bottom of the screen and scrollback. If the new character and current character have the same width, just replaces the character If the new character has a width of 1 and the old character has a width of 2, removes the trailing character If the new character has a width of 2 and the old character has a width of 1, adds a trailing character Moves the mouse {$newCharWidth} spaces to the right, stopping if it has reached the end of the existing text
      Parameters:
      text - the new character to add
      Returns:
      true if the text was overwritten, false if the cursor is not at the bottom line

    • setBackgroundColourAtCursorPos

      public boolean setBackgroundColourAtCursorPos(Colour colour)
      Sets the background colour at the mouse cursor's current position only if the cursor is at the bottom of the screen and scrollback. Does not move the mouse position
      Parameters:
      colour - the new background colour of the cell
      Returns:
      true if the text was overwritten, false if the cursor is not at the bottom line

    • setForegroundColourAtCursorPos

      public boolean setForegroundColourAtCursorPos(Colour colour)
      Sets the foreground colour at the mouse cursor's current position only if the cursor is at the bottom of the screen and scrollback. Does not move the mouse position
      Parameters:
      colour - the new foreground colour of the cell
      Returns:
      true if the text was overwritten, false if the cursor is not at the bottom line

    • setStyleAtCursorPos

      public boolean setStyleAtCursorPos(Style style, boolean val)
      Sets the style at the mouse cursor's current position only if the cursor is at the bottom of the screen and scrollback. Does not move the mouse position
      Parameters:
      style - the new style of the cell
      Returns:
      true if the text was overwritten, false if the cursor is not at the bottom line

    • clearLine

      public boolean clearLine()
      Clears the last line in the buffer and rebuilds the screen if necessary. Only works if the cursor is at the bottom line
      Returns:
      true if the line was cleared, false if the cursor is not at the bottom line

    • fillLineWithChar

      public boolean fillLineWithChar(CharacterCell fill)
      Fills a terminal line with the specified character starting from the cursor's current screen position e.g. if the screen's cursor position is 1 and the width is 4, 3 cells will be filled Exits early if the cursor is not at the bottom line Moves a cursor along with the fill Since the input is a CharacterCell, the colours and style of the character can be specified
      Returns:
      true if the line was cleared, false if the cursor is not at the bottom line

    • fillLineWithChar

      public boolean fillLineWithChar(Character fill)
      Fills a terminal line with the specified character starting from the cursor's current screen position e.g. if the screen's cursor position is 1 and the width is 4, 3 cells will be filled Exits early if the cursor is not at the bottom line Moves a cursor along with the fill Since the input is a Character, the colours and style of the character will be set to the TerminalBuffer's defaults
      Returns:
      true if the line was cleared, false if the cursor is not at the bottom line

    • getCharAtCursorPos

      public Character getCharAtCursorPos()
      Returns:
      the character stored in the cell at the current cursor's position if the cursor is not beyond the end of the line

    • getBackgroundColourAtCursorPos

      public Colour getBackgroundColourAtCursorPos()
      Returns:
      the background colour stored in the cell at the current cursor's position if the cursor is not beyond the end of the line

    • getForegroundColourAtCursorPos

      public Colour getForegroundColourAtCursorPos()
      Returns:
      the foreground colour stored in the cell at the current cursor's position if the cursor is not beyond the end of the line

    • getStyleAtCursorPos

      public boolean[] getStyleAtCursorPos()
      Returns:
      the styles stored in the cell at the current cursor's position if the cursor is not beyond the end of the line

    • getStyleAtCursorPos

      public boolean getStyleAtCursorPos(Style style)
      Parameters:
      style - the style to check for having been set
      Returns:
      if the provided style has been applied to the current cell

    • printScreenLine

      public String printScreenLine()
      Returns:
      the entire terminal line the cursor is currently on as a String

    • printScrollLine

      public String printScrollLine()
      Returns:
      the entire logical line the cursor is currently on as a String

    • printScreenContents

      public String printScreenContents()
      Returns:
      entire screen content as a string. Does not handle colours or styles

    • printScrollbackContents

      public String printScrollbackContents()
      Returns:
      the entire scrollback content as a string. Does not handle colours or styles
